subject: feat(intents): add intent tagging to modules
body:
Intent tags let the creator categorize what a module clause is meant to do.
Six defaults: obligation, permission, restriction, definition, process,
protection. Custom intents supported via the same label system as tones.

Generalized ToneSelector to accept configurable defaults, label, and
placeholder props so it can be reused for both tones (notes/proposals)
and intents (modules).

New files:
- src/components/modules/IntentSelector.tsx: wrapper with intent defaults

Modified:
- ToneSelector: now accepts defaults, label, customPlaceholder props
- ModuleEditor: IntentSelector below content, saves immediately on change
- ModuleViewer: read-only intent pills
- ModuleCard: intent pills in agreement module list
- Module type: added intent: string[]
- useModules: updateModule accepts intent field
- constants/tones.ts: DEFAULT_INTENTS, MAX_INTENTS_PER_MODULE

Database:
- Migration adds intent text[] to sancio_modules

Tags: feature, intents, modules, ui
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: neutral
Epic: tone-tagging
Semver: minor
